summary.intent: Perform an adversarial code review of story 5-2 (AI Metrics Dashboard Page) using the bmad-code-review skill, then apply all identified fixes.
summary.outcome: Review completed with 3 parallel agents (Blind Hunter, Edge Case Hunter, Acceptance Auditor); 16 patches applied, 2 design decisions resolved, 7 items deferred, and story status advanced to 'done'.

repo: The bmad-code-review skill runs three parallel agents: Blind Hunter (adversarial, diff-only), Edge Case Hunter (boundary analysis with repo access), and Acceptance Auditor (spec compliance against ACs).
Story status lifecycle: review → done, tracked in _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml.
Dev Notes in story spec files contain all utility function signatures, chart patterns, and anti-patterns that override default implementation choices.
Astro build-time frontmatter executes at build time, so runtime JSON schema drift (e.g. null prompt_txt) crashes the build, not just the UI.

code.finding: resolve('./src/data/...') resolves relative to process.cwd(), not the file; should use new URL('../data/...', import.meta.url) for build portability.
JSON.parse(readFileSync(...)) has no try/catch; malformed cache file crashes the entire build with unhandled SyntaxError.
Cache-missing UI renders in all modes, not guarded by import.meta.env.DEV as AC3 requires.
classifyPromptType: whitespace-only strings (e.g. '   ') pass the empty-string guard and are misclassified as Free-form instead of Continuation.
computeCheckpointCadenceMinutes: returns N values with index 0 always = 0; callers expecting N-1 deltas get a spurious zero bar. Fixed by slicing away index 0 in AiTemporalSection.
computeCheckpointCadenceMinutes: invalid commit_date produces NaN via new Date(invalid).getTime(), which propagates unguarded into chart data arrays.
computeRollingAverage: window <= 0 causes division by zero returning NaN for all values; function is exported so external callers are at risk.

Metric 3 denominator guard Math.max(agent_lines, 1) silently distorts human modification rate when agent_lines === 0, producing nonsensical percentages. Fixed to return 0 for zero-agent-line checkpoints.
Metric 3/4 no-data fallback used style="..." as a literal string attribute value instead of real CSS, making the fallback div invisible.
DOUGHNUT_COLORS has only 6 entries; if more than 6 distinct model names exist, extra chart segments receive undefined backgroundColor and render transparent.
classifyPromptType called with potential null/undefined prompt_txt; null.trim() throws TypeError crashing Astro build-time frontmatter.
Only Inter 400 weight imported via @fontsource, but dashboard uses font-semibold (600) and font-medium (500) classes, causing browser to synthesize bold typography.

aggregateBmadCommands test computed expected array using the same runtime comparator as the implementation, making the assertion self-referentially vacuous; expected value should be a hardcoded literal.

workflow: Blind Hunter agent without file-read access can produce false-positive 'stub' findings when given abbreviated file content in the prompt; always cross-check against files actually read.
Running three review agents in parallel (Blind Hunter, Edge Case Hunter, Acceptance Auditor) significantly increases finding coverage without context window bloat.

lightweight-charts requires strictly ascending unique Unix-second timestamps; any two checkpoints < 1 second apart or with identical commit_date will crash setData() — deduplication must be applied before every setData() call.
The 'decision-needed' pattern (DN1, DN2) is effective for surfacing metric-semantic design choices that require product input before patching.

friction: Blind Hunter flagged AiQualitySection, AiBehavioralSection, AiTokenSection, and AiTemporalSection as 'critical stubs' because it only received partial file content; required manual triage to dismiss ~4 false-positive critical findings.
AiTokenSection required multiple read-then-edit cycles (4+ reads) due to edit conflicts or stale context, slowing patch application.
AiQualitySection and AiTemporalSection similarly required intermediate reads before edits could succeed.
open_items: AC3 compliance: cache-missing UI should be gated by import.meta.env.DEV; currently renders in production too (blocked by AC2 build guard but not explicitly enforced in code).
canvas elements have no explicit width/height HTML attributes, causing CLS flicker on initial layout pass before Chart.js resizes them.
cache.meta.checkpointCount is never validated against actual cache.checkpoints.length; stale meta count can contradict rendered chart data.
classifyFileLayer misclassifies paths under docs/components/ as 'components' instead of 'docs' due to substring-match ordering.
DARK_CHART_OPTIONS.legend.display hardcoded to false; callers that need legend must remember to override it — should be a factory function or the legend block should be omitted from the base config.
m3Values division guard (Math.max(agent_lines, 1)) semantics: now returns 0 for zero-agent-line checkpoints, but no tooltip or annotation flags this to the viewer.

Metric 19 single-checkpoint edge case: computeCheckpointCadenceMinutes returns [0] for a single checkpoint, but after stripping index 0 the chart shows empty with no 'single checkpoint — no cadence measurable' message.
